sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 18:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section III, List of Answers on Terminology 18:1
items.html: An initiation existence from absence is called “creation.” The coarseness in the vessels could not be revealed in the world of Atzilut, but only from the bottom of the world of Atzilut downwards. Therefore, from that place downward it is called Beria, after the coarseness that appeared. The coarseness is the will to receive in the created being, existence from absence, for everything in reality is regarded as emerging from Him existence from existence. This is because one cannot give what one does not have, except for the will to receive, which by necessity is not in Him, for from whom would He receive? (Part 3, Chapter 2, item 3)
